items.correct: Viruses are not classed as fully living because they cannot carry out life processes alone and only reproduce inside host cells.
items.misconception: Viruses are living organisms just like bacteria.
items.random_wrong: Viruses are a type of large bacterium.
items.why: Students group viruses with bacteria, not realising viruses are non-cellular and need a host.

items.correct: No - bacteria are prokaryotes; their DNA is a loop free in the cytoplasm, with no membrane-bound nucleus.
items.misconception: Bacteria have a nucleus like animal and plant cells.
items.random_wrong: Bacteria have no DNA at all.
items.why: Students apply the eukaryotic cell plan to bacteria.

items.q: How do fungi obtain their food?
items.correct: Fungi feed by saprotrophic nutrition: they secrete enzymes onto food and absorb the digested products.
items.misconception: Fungi make their own food by photosynthesis like plants.
items.random_wrong: Fungi swallow food and digest it internally like animals.
items.why: Students assume any non-animal organism either photosynthesises or digests food as animals do.

items.q: The seven characteristics of living organisms include all of these except:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: combustion. “respiration” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Bacteria are classified as prokaryotes because their cells:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: have no membrane-bound nucleus. “contain chloroplasts” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: secreting enzymes and absorbing digested food
items.why: The answer examiners want is: secreting enzymes and absorbing digested food. “photosynthesis in chloroplasts” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Viruses are not considered fully living because they:
items.correct: can only reproduce inside a host cell
items.why: Correct answer: can only reproduce inside a host cell. “are made of many cells” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
